title: Asuka fanservice SPLIT
wikitext:
[]: Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 19, 2013 1:11 pm ElMariachi wrote: What are you talking about, is that about the 14 years time skip where she didn't aged?

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Thu Dec 19, 2013 1:14 pm ^ Oh, the famous "slutsuit" as it has been nicknamed with such good taste! So according to that interpretation it's the Rebuild equivalent of the hospital scene, except that this time we don't get to see tits, and the goo involved is blue and glowing instead of white!

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Thu Dec 19, 2013 5:45 pm ^ Oh she already realized in 2.0 that she needs help from time to time and that there isn't anything shameful about it, that was the whole point of the pillow talk with Shinji and her later phone call to Misato just before EVA-03's activation test. I'm just saying that maybe the later events (Bardiel + coma + angelic infection + whatever the fuck happened to the world) made her relapse into the same habits than during her introduction as wanting to be the only one who fight, only worse, and that Mari accepted to step down as a way to help Asuka to overcome her fear of not being good enough to pilot. Obviously Asuka learned the lesson, you just have to see Q, where she doesn't hesitate to call for Mari's support when she's in a bad situation, to confirm it. That served just as a way to speculate onto what could have been Mari's role "behind the scene", during the time skip, and she strike me as the type of person who would make excellent warrior therapists for the rest of the cast, she already showed shades of these tendencies both in 2.0 and 3.0 when she talked to Shinji.

[]: Post by Reichu » Thu Dec 19, 2013 6:39 pm DarthGendo wrote: Whoa whoa whoa Reichu, slow the buck down. (SNIIIIIIIP)

[]: Post by Lord ikari shinji » Thu Dec 19, 2013 7:19 pm Reichu wrote: Firstly, I did not come up with that idea regarding the "metatextual" significance of the slutsuit, and secondly, if the hypothesis were true, any sexism in question would belong to Eva 's masterminds and hence be open for discussion. However, I don't actually see how criticizing a specific subset of the Japanese male population (the one that actually is know to withdraw into their man-child caves and fetishize 2D teenage girls) can be interpreted as sexist... unless you're just doing this for the sake of being argumentative.

[]: Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Dec 19, 2013 8:01 pm Lord ikari Shinji wrote: honestly, that Asuka fan service suit was unnecessary/ fan service {imo} is for the trashier animes/mangas to keep viewers reading/watching (i.e. high school of the dead) it does hurt to have it{1-2 moments} but just not too much.

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Fri Dec 20, 2013 4:29 am Guy Nacks wrote: You know what was really unnecessary? That three second pan over her taint while she's in bed. Fan-service suit really didn't seem all that major compared to that, that shot seemed a little TOO voyeurisic.

[]: Post by Monk Ed » Fri Dec 20, 2013 4:42 am I must reiterate every time I see this mentioned, but I didn't even notice the far-too-often-mentioned crotch shot my first time watching Ha. Get on my level, people.

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Dec 20, 2013 4:44 am ElMariachi wrote: Oh, happy to learn that I wasn't the only one bothered by that shot

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Fri Dec 20, 2013 5:12 am Mr. Tines wrote: Shimapan ? Lately? Only if you mean over the last decade or so (classic pantsu anime like Najica and Agent Aika may have been into the plain white cotton, but stripes were hardly new by the time they appeared prominently in Strike Witches ).

[]: Post by Squigsquasher » Fri Dec 20, 2013 6:56 am ElMariachi wrote: I hate ecchi animes and manga with a passion, even if they have other interesting points(that's why I never saw High School of the Dead even though I've hear that it has over-the-top action and gore), I think it just spoils the potential of a story and that the creators think of me as a primate who can't concentrate in a story if I don't see boobz for more than five minutes. So yeah, I never noticed this whole thing with "Shimapan"(seriously, there's even a name for that?!) until that crotch shot in 2.0, and after that I noticed that it's almost everywhere. From where that passion for these kind of panties comes from?

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Fri Dec 20, 2013 7:45 am ^ The night after the Sahaquiel battle, when Asuka goes to Shinji's room and they have the "pillow talk", she's wearing stripped panties, you can't miss them(and the movie makes damn sure that you won't miss them!)

[]: Post by Giji Shinka » Fri Dec 20, 2013 8:11 am ElMariachi wrote: ^ (and the movie makes damn sure that you won't miss them!)

[]: Post by Chuckman » Fri Dec 20, 2013 10:23 am The taint shot symbolizes her vulnerability. She's rolling around trying to cover herself up but she's exposed the whole time. Exposed why? Small Japanese blankets. She's made vulnerable by immersion in a culture not her own. The movie has very little time to devote to her characterization so it brilliantly and subtly uses ecchi fanservice tropes to tell a story. Her visual arc constantly references nudity and fanservice. When Asuka is naked she has power. She kicks Shinji in the face. The slutsuit represents male force constraining and "presenting" female skin, creating a structure of power by contextualization of nudity. Asuka's striped panties are a shackle, a shield to contain her. They are shown in detail not to tittilate but to prove how much we fear her powerful pussy, source of her awe and mystery. She is also trussed up as a sacrifice and fed to a monster. Asuka's scantily clad form becomes angelic (wings of light as she descends into the angel's grip) symbolizing ascent. The final images of her in 2.0 are containment within phallic objects (the plug, the coffin). Asuka is put inside a giant metal penis, which Yui eats. The consumption of the girl with a crush on Shinji inside a big metal cock is a symbolic murder of any aspirations he may have to leave the comfort of Yui's breast. When Unit 01 chomps on the entry plug, Yui is devouring Shinji's cock and consuming his independence while murdering a potential mate. Hence the suggestive posing and position in the Eva in the shot, which heavily suggests a pregnant belly. Yui as monster-mother. Asuka survives the ordeal but comes out wounded. Her eye is again damaged, symbolically linking the act of Yui chomping the plug to Yui refusing to render aid to the competition for her son's affections in End of Evangelion. More importantly the theme of wounds to arm, side, and eye is preserved. Asuka is reborn in the death of her burgeoning romantic attraction to Shinji to become a goddess of wisdom and warfare, hence her immortality. The fact that her eye is not actually missing and contains some yet unexplained power indicates that humanity is not prepared for her transcendence and must sheathe it out of fear. The logistical reasons why she begins the film in her taped up suit are irrelevant. In casting it off she casts off her wounds, and thus her connection to Shinji. Hence rage in the latter portion of the film. There is a thematic echo of EoE. The world is broken because Shinji and Asuka are broken. The ending suggests a rebirth of her feelings for him. A rebuilding? Perhaps. Perhaps not.

[]: Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Dec 20, 2013 11:00 am Chuckman wrote: The taint shot symbolizes her vulnerability.

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Fri Dec 20, 2013 11:09 am @ chuckman : is it normal that I'm not sure if I should take seriously half of the things you said? Yui can't have an connection to Asuka being mauling, since it wasn't her but the Dummy System which acted and desgrohed EVA-03. Yui even violently rejects the Dummy System next time NERV tries to use it.

[]: Post by Chuckman » Fri Dec 20, 2013 11:52 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: Thiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiiis. Asuka actually has two different pairs of striped panties in 2.0. When do the other ones show up? SPOILER: Show

[]: Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Dec 20, 2013 12:01 pm My one and only disagreement with you is that the duffle bag is actually blocking off the closet. the doors to the room are in the top right between the table and the book shelf.

[]: Post by Giji Shinka » Fri Dec 20, 2013 12:26 pm @Chuckman I died. Way too good stuff.

[]: Post by ElMariachi » Fri Dec 20, 2013 12:30 pm Errr... Chuckman, don't you think you're slightly overanalyzing the scene? I know Anno loves to put symbolism in his scenes, but that's overkill, especially since the alleged message of the symbolism(Asuka shields herself behind a persona) is already illustrated by her dialog and the puppet's presence during that scene! Chuckman wrote: You disprove your own point. If Yui wanted to stop Asuka's mauling she could have. Yui's guilt or presence or whatever is irrelevant to the symbol of the plug with Asuka inside in the mouth of a grotesque demonic monster-mother. Anyway plot /= symbolism. Sometimes they can even be mutually exclusive.
